1.arabic: بسم الله الرحمن الرحيم اقتربت الساعة وانشق القمر
1.english: The Hour has come near, and the moon has split [in two].
1.transliteration: Iqtarabatis Saa'atu wsan shaqqal qamar

1.arabic: وإن يروا آية يعرضوا ويقولوا سحر مستمر
1.english: And if they see a miracle, they turn away and say, "Passing magic."
1.transliteration: Wa iny yaraw aayatany yu'ridoo wa yaqooloo sihrum mustamirr

1.arabic: وكذبوا واتبعوا أهواءهم ۚ وكل أمر مستقر
1.english: And they denied and followed their inclinations. But for every matter is a [time of] settlement.
1.transliteration: Wa kazzaboo wattaba'ooo ahwaaa'ahum; wa kullu amrim mustaqirr

1.arabic: ولقد جاءهم من الأنباء ما فيه مزدجر
1.english: And there has already come to them of information that in which there is deterrence -
1.transliteration: Wa laqad jaaa'ahum minal ambaaa'i maa feehi muzdajar

1.arabic: حكمة بالغة ۖ فما تغن النذر
1.english: Extensive wisdom - but warning does not avail [them].
1.transliteration: Hikmatum baalighatun famaa tughnin nuzur

1.arabic: فتول عنهم ۘ يوم يدع الداع إلى شيء نكر
1.english: So leave them, [O Muhammad]. The Day the Caller calls to something forbidding,
1.transliteration: Fatawalla 'anhum; yawma yad'ud daa'i ilaa shai 'in nukur

1.arabic: خشعا أبصارهم يخرجون من الأجداث كأنهم جراد منتشر
1.english: Their eyes humbled, they will emerge from the graves as if they were locusts spreading,
1.transliteration: khushsha'an absaaruhum yakrujoona minal ajdaasi ka annahum jaraadum muntashir

1.arabic: مهطعين إلى الداع ۖ يقول الكافرون هذا يوم عسر
1.english: Racing ahead toward the Caller. The disbelievers will say, "This is a difficult Day."
1.transliteration: Muhti'eena ilad daa'i yaqoolul kafiroona haazaa yawmun 'asir

1.arabic: ۞ كذبت قبلهم قوم نوح فكذبوا عبدنا وقالوا مجنون وازدجر
1.english: The people of Noah denied before them, and they denied Our servant and said, "A madman," and he was repelled.
1.transliteration: Kazzabat qablahum qawmu Noohin fakazzaboo 'abdanaa wa qaaloo majnoo nunw wazdujir

1.arabic: فدعا ربه أني مغلوب فانتصر
1.english: So he invoked his Lord, "Indeed, I am overpowered, so help."
1.transliteration: Fada'aa Rabbahooo annee maghloobun fantasir

1.arabic: ففتحنا أبواب السماء بماء منهمر
1.english: Then We opened the gates of the heaven with rain pouring down
1.transliteration: Fafatahnaaa abwaabas sa maaa'i bimaa'im munhamir

1.arabic: وفجرنا الأرض عيونا فالتقى الماء على أمر قد قدر
1.english: And caused the earth to burst with springs, and the waters met for a matter already predestined.
1.transliteration: Wa fajjamal arda 'uyoonan faltaqal maaa'u 'alaaa amrin qad qudir

1.arabic: وحملناه على ذات ألواح ودسر
1.english: And We carried him on a [construction of] planks and nails,
1.transliteration: Wa hamalnaahu 'alaa zaati alwaahinw wa dusur

1.arabic: تجري بأعيننا جزاء لمن كان كفر
1.english: Sailing under Our observation as reward for he who had been denied.
1.transliteration: Tajree bi a'yuninaa jazaaa 'al liman kaana kufir

1.arabic: ولقد تركناها آية فهل من مدكر
1.english: And We left it as a sign, so is there any who will remember?
1.transliteration: Wa laqat taraknaahaad aayatan fahal mim muddakir

1.arabic: فكيف كان عذابي ونذر
1.english: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.
1.transliteration: Fakaifa kaana 'azaabee wa nuzur

1.arabic: ولقد يسرنا القرآن للذكر فهل من مدكر
1.english: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
1.transliteration: Wa laqad yassamal Quraana liz zikri fahal mimmuddakir

1.arabic: كذبت عاد فكيف كان عذابي ونذر
1.english: 'Aad denied; and how [severe] were My punishment and warning.
1.transliteration: Kazzabat 'Aadun fakaifa kaana 'azaabee wa nuzur

1.arabic: إنا أرسلنا عليهم ريحا صرصرا في يوم نحس مستمر
1.english: Indeed, We sent upon them a screaming wind on a day of continuous misfortune,
1.transliteration: Innaa arsalnaa 'alaihim reehan sarsaran fee Yawmi nahsim mustamirr

1.arabic: تنزع الناس كأنهم أعجاز نخل منقعر
1.english: Extracting the people as if they were trunks of palm trees uprooted.
1.transliteration: Tanzi;un naasa ka anna huma'jaazu nakhlim munqa'ir

1.arabic: فكيف كان عذابي ونذر
1.english: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.
1.transliteration: 'Fakaifa kaana 'azaabee wa nuzur

1.arabic: ولقد يسرنا القرآن للذكر فهل من مدكر
1.english: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
1.transliteration: Wa laqad yassamal Quraana liz zikri fahal mim muddakir

1.arabic: كذبت ثمود بالنذر
1.english: Thamud denied the warning
1.transliteration: Kazzabat Samoodu binnuzur

1.arabic: فقالوا أبشرا منا واحدا نتبعه إنا إذا لفي ضلال وسعر
1.english: And said, "Is it one human being among us that we should follow? Indeed, we would then be in error and madness.
1.transliteration: Faqaalooo a-basharam minnaa waahidan nattabi'uhooo innaa izal lafee dalaalinw wa su'ur

1.arabic: أألقي الذكر عليه من بيننا بل هو كذاب أشر
1.english: Has the message been sent down upon him from among us? Rather, he is an insolent liar."
1.transliteration: 'A-ulqiyaz zikru 'alaihi mim baininaa bal huwa kazzaabun ashir

1.arabic: سيعلمون غدا من الكذاب الأشر
1.english: They will know tomorrow who is the insolent liar.
1.transliteration: Sa-ya'lamoona ghadam manil kazzaabul ashir

1.arabic: إنا مرسلو الناقة فتنة لهم فارتقبهم واصطبر
1.english: Indeed, We are sending the she-camel as trial for them, so watch them and be patient.
1.transliteration: Innaa mursilun naaqati fitnatal lahum fartaqibhum wastabir

1.arabic: ونبئهم أن الماء قسمة بينهم ۖ كل شرب محتضر
1.english: And inform them that the water is shared between them, each [day of] drink attended [by turn].
1.transliteration: Wa nabbi'hum annal maaa'a qismatum bainahum kullu shirbim muhtadar

1.arabic: فنادوا صاحبهم فتعاطى فعقر
1.english: But they called their companion, and he dared and hamstrung [her].
1.transliteration: Fanaadaw saahibahum fata'aataa fa'aqar

1.arabic: فكيف كان عذابي ونذر
1.english: And how [severe] were My punishment and warning.
1.transliteration: Fakaifa kaana 'azaabee wa nuzur

1.arabic: إنا أرسلنا عليهم صيحة واحدة فكانوا كهشيم المحتظر
1.english: Indeed, We sent upon them one blast from the sky, and they became like the dry twig fragments of an [animal] pen.
1.transliteration: Innaaa arsalnaa 'alaihim saihatanw waahidatan fakaano kahasheemil muhtazir

1.arabic: ولقد يسرنا القرآن للذكر فهل من مدكر
1.english: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
1.transliteration: Wa laqad yassarnal quraana liz zikri fahal mim muddakir

1.arabic: كذبت قوم لوط بالنذر
1.english: The people of Lot denied the warning.
1.transliteration: Kazzabat qawmu lootim binnuzur

1.arabic: إنا أرسلنا عليهم حاصبا إلا آل لوط ۖ نجيناهم بسحر
1.english: Indeed, We sent upon them a storm of stones, except the family of Lot - We saved them before dawn
1.transliteration: Innaa arsalnaa 'alaihim haasiban illaaa aala Loot najjainaahum bisahar

1.arabic: نعمة من عندنا ۚ كذلك نجزي من شكر
1.english: As favor from us. Thus do We reward he who is grateful.
1.transliteration: Ni'matam min 'indinaa; kazaalika najzee man shakar

1.arabic: ولقد أنذرهم بطشتنا فتماروا بالنذر
1.english: And he had already warned them of Our assault, but they disputed the warning.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad anzarahum batshatanaa fatamaaraw binnuzur

1.arabic: ولقد راودوه عن ضيفه فطمسنا أعينهم فذوقوا عذابي ونذر
1.english: And they had demanded from him his guests, but We obliterated their eyes, [saying], "Taste My punishment and warning."
1.transliteration: Wa laqad raawadoohu 'andaifee fatamasnaaa a'yunahum fazooqoo 'azaabee wa nuzur

1.arabic: ولقد صبحهم بكرة عذاب مستقر
1.english: And there came upon them by morning an abiding punishment.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad sabbahahum bukratan 'azaabum mustaqirr

1.arabic: فذوقوا عذابي ونذر
1.english: So taste My punishment and warning.
1.transliteration: Fazooqoo 'azaabee wa nuzur

1.arabic: ولقد يسرنا القرآن للذكر فهل من مدكر
1.english: And We have certainly made the Qur'an easy for remembrance, so is there any who will remember?
1.transliteration: Wa laqad yassarnal Quraana liz zikri fahal mim muddakir

1.arabic: ولقد جاء آل فرعون النذر
1.english: And there certainly came to the people of Pharaoh warning.
1.transliteration: Wa laqad jaaa'a Aala Fir'awnan nuzur

1.arabic: كذبوا بآياتنا كلها فأخذناهم أخذ عزيز مقتدر
1.english: They denied Our signs, all of them, so We seized them with a seizure of one Exalted in Might and Perfect in Ability.
1.transliteration: Kazzaboo bi Aayaatinaa kullihaa fa akhaznaahum akhza 'azeezim muqtadir

1.arabic: أكفاركم خير من أولئكم أم لكم براءة في الزبر
1.english: Are your disbelievers better than those [former ones], or have you immunity in the scripture?
1.transliteration: Akuffaarukum khairum min ulaaa'ikum am lakum baraaa'atun fiz Zubur

1.arabic: أم يقولون نحن جميع منتصر
1.english: Or do they say, "We are an assembly supporting [each other]"?
1.transliteration: Am yaqooloona nahnu jamee'um muntasir

1.arabic: سيهزم الجمع ويولون الدبر
1.english: [Their] assembly will be defeated, and they will turn their backs [in retreat].
1.transliteration: Sa yuhzamul jam'u wa yuwalloonad dubur

1.arabic: بل الساعة موعدهم والساعة أدهى وأمر
1.english: But the Hour is their appointment [for due punishment], and the Hour is more disastrous and more bitter.
1.transliteration: Balis Saa'atu maw'iduhum was Saa'atu adhaa wa amarr

1.arabic: إن المجرمين في ضلال وسعر
1.english: Indeed, the criminals are in error and madness.
1.transliteration: Innal mujrimeena fee dalaalinw wa su'ur

1.arabic: يوم يسحبون في النار على وجوههم ذوقوا مس سقر
1.english: The Day they are dragged into the Fire on their faces [it will be said], "Taste the touch of Saqar."
1.transliteration: Yawma yushaboona fin Naari'alaa wujoohimim zooqoo massa saqar

1.arabic: إنا كل شيء خلقناه بقدر
1.english: Indeed, all things We created with predestination.
1.transliteration: Innaa kulla shai'in khalaqnaahu biqadar

1.arabic: وما أمرنا إلا واحدة كلمح بالبصر
1.english: And Our command is but one, like a glance of the eye.
1.transliteration: Wa maaa amrunaaa illaa waahidatun kalamhim bilbasar

1.arabic: ولقد أهلكنا أشياعكم فهل من مدكر
1.english: And We have already destroyed your kinds, so is there any who will remember?
1.transliteration: Wa laqad ahlaknaaa ashyaa'akum fahal mim muddakir

1.arabic: وكل شيء فعلوه في الزبر
1.english: And everything they did is in written records.
1.transliteration: Wa kullu shai'in fa'aloohu fiz Zubur

1.arabic: وكل صغير وكبير مستطر
1.english: And every small and great [thing] is inscribed.
1.transliteration: Wa kullu sagheerinw wa kabeerim mustatar

1.arabic: إن المتقين في جنات ونهر
1.english: Indeed, the righteous will be among gardens and rivers,
1.transliteration: Innal muttaqeena fee jannaatinw wa nahar

1.arabic: في مقعد صدق عند مليك مقتدر
1.english: In a seat of honor near a Sovereign, Perfect in Ability.
1.transliteration: Fee maq'adi sidqin 'inda Maleekim Muqtadir
